items.q: Was the Elizabethan Settlement of 1559 a fully Protestant church?
items.correct: It was a deliberate compromise: Protestant in doctrine but keeping some traditional structures and ceremonies to appeal to moderates.
items.misconception: Elizabeth made England a strictly Calvinist Protestant state in 1559.
items.random_wrong: The 1559 Settlement restored the Pope's authority.
items.why: Students assume a hardline Protestant church; Elizabeth's 'middle way' sought broad acceptance, frustrating both Catholics and Puritans.

items.q: Why is Mary I remembered as 'Bloody Mary'?
items.correct: She burned around 280 Protestants as heretics during her attempt to restore Catholicism, fixing the grim nickname in Protestant memory.
items.misconception: Mary I executed people for treason in the Civil War.
items.why: Students misplace her; Mary preceded Elizabeth, and the nickname comes from the religious burnings, later amplified by Protestant propaganda.

items.q: Did the Armada's defeat in 1588 end the threat from Spain?
items.correct: It was a major victory but war with Spain continued for years, with further armadas and ongoing naval conflict into the 1590s.
items.misconception: The 1588 defeat ended all Spanish threats to England immediately.
items.random_wrong: The Armada successfully landed troops in England.
items.why: Learners treat 1588 as final; the Anglo-Spanish war dragged on, and the victory was as much symbolic as decisive.

items.q: Which monarch tried to restore Catholicism between 1553 and 1558?
items.why: Correct answer: Mary I. “Edward VI” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What did the 1559 Religious Settlement establish?
items.correct: A Protestant Church with the monarch as Supreme Governor
items.why: Correct answer: A Protestant Church with the monarch as Supreme Governor. “Full Catholic restoration” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What happened to the Spanish Armada in 1588?
items.correct: It was defeated by the English fleet and storms
items.why: It's It was defeated by the English fleet and storms. “It successfully invaded England” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What did the 1601 Poor Law make parishes responsible for?
items.why: Correct answer: Relieving the poor. “Collecting Ship Money” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was the Spanish Armada defeated?
items.why: The real figure is 1588 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
